mnemonics.title: Embedded System Components
mnemonics.content: CPM: Central Processor (CPU), Peripheral devices, Memory (RAM/ROM)

mnemonics.title: Key Characteristics
mnemonics.content: DRART: Dedicated, Real-time, Affordable (cost-effective), Reliable, Tiny (compact)

mnemonics.title: Embedded System Classification
mnemonics.content: SMS: Small-scale, Medium-scale, Sophisticated (based on complexity)

mnemonics.title: Real-Time Types
mnemonics.content: HS-SS: Hard Real-Time (Safety-critical) vs Soft Real-Time (Speed-sensitive)

mnemonics.title: I/O Devices
mnemonics.content: SADA: Sensors (Input) → ADC → Digital Processing → DAC → Actuators (Output)

acronyms.usage: Single chip containing complete computing system (CPU, memory, I/O)
Integrated processor with built-in memory and peripherals
Interface between analog sensors/actuators and digital processor
Volatile memory for temporary data storage during operation
Non-volatile memory for storing permanent firmware/program
Non-volatile memory that can be reprogrammed electrically
Interface for communication between processor and external devices
Programmable pins for digital input/output operations
Reliability metric measuring average time between system failures
Code executed in response to hardware interrupt events

visualTips.visualization: Draw a central box (processor) connected to three boxes (Memory, Input, Output). Add arrows showing data flow between them. Label arrows with 'data bus' and 'address bus'. Add power supply as a common line to all components.
Pyramid with 3 levels: Top (Registers - fastest/smallest), Middle (RAM - medium), Bottom (ROM/Flash - slowest/largest). Label each level with access speed and typical size.
Draw processor in center. Connect memory on one side with address/data/control lines. Connect I/O devices on another side through interface controllers. Show clock and power supply lines to all.
Two circles: Hard RT (strict deadline - draw red boundary), Soft RT (flexible deadline - draw green boundary). Place examples: Airbag = Hard, Video player = Soft.
Rectangle representing MCU with internal blocks: CPU, RAM, ROM, Timer, I/O Ports, ADC, UART. Show external connections to sensors, actuators, and programming interface.

analogies.concept: Embedded System vs General Purpose Computer
analogies.analogy: Like a calculator (embedded) vs a personal computer (general-purpose). Calculator does one thing perfectly; PC does many things adequately.
The brain in our body - processes information, controls all organs, but needs memory (like hippocampus) and input/output (like senses and muscles).
Firmware is like the instinct/inborn knowledge we're born with (permanent). Application software is like learned skills we acquire later (installable).
Like traffic lights - they always turn green/yellow/red in predictable sequence and timing, not random or adaptive.
Like a guard dog that barks to wake you if something goes wrong at night. It monitors and resets the system if it 'sleeps' (hangs) too long.
Like moving from a house with separate rooms (discrete components) to a studio apartment (integrated) - everything in one compact space.
